student_answer: Step 1: Start from methyl 5-methyl-1H-indole-2-carboxylate; protect the indole N with (Boc)2O/DMAP (or NaH, (Boc)2O) to give methyl 5-methyl-1-Boc-indole-2-carboxylate. | Step 2: Nitrate the ring selectively at C-6 with HNO3/Ac2O or HNO3/H2SO4 at low temperature to form methyl 5-methyl-6-nitro-1-Boc-indole-2-carboxylate. | Step 3: Benzylic bromination of the 5-methyl group with NBS, AIBN (or hν) gives methyl 5-(bromomethyl)-6-nitro-1-Boc-indole-2-carboxylate. | Step 4: Displace bromide with potassium phthalimide in DMF to install the N-phthalimidomethyl group, affording methyl 5-(phthalimidomethyl)-6-nitro-1-Boc-indole-2-carboxylate, i.e. COC(=O)c1cc2cc(CN3C(=O)c4ccccc4C3=O)cc([N+](=O)[O-])c2n1C(=O)OC(C)(C)C.
